device: QB2 / bh-qbge-06 / P150x4 (4 Blackhole, TP=4, (1,4) mesh)
install: VLLM_TARGET_DEVICE=empty uv pip install -e . --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu --index-strategy unsafe-best-match ; uv pip install -e plugins/vllm-tt-plugin
note: Fresh, project-matching vLLM built against the CURRENT tt-metal. NOT the stale ghcr image 0.14.0-80180b9-7678b70.
max_model_len_note: 1024 is the inner-loop bring-up value (fast KV/build). Context contract advertises 262144; unchanged for final headline evidence.
datatype: bf16 weights + bf16 KV + bf16 CCL (full-model policy)

startup.model_build: 30-layer 26B on (1,4) mesh, warmup skipped (block-diffusion warms lazily)

live_47488_blocker_prepatch.error: RuntimeError: shape '[1]' is invalid for input of size 256
live_47488_blocker_prepatch.meaning: model committed a 256-token canvas block; runner assumed one committed token per step (#47488).

device_recovery.signature: TT_THROW llrt.cpp:566 assert_active_ethernet_cores_to_reset (hard-killed EngineCore left eth un-reset)
device_recovery.note: prefer graceful SIGTERM shutdown of the server to avoid re-dirtying the device

live_request_postpatch.text_empty_reason: committed argmax canvas placed EOS at position 0 -> vLLM stops at first token, skip_special_tokens -> empty. Known RUN-first #48291 fidelity behavior (bf16/MoE/TP=4 argmax commits EOS-heavy blocks). NOT a serving regression.
live_request_postpatch.metrics_note: block-diffusion cost profile is per-block; 1000/mean_tpot_ms is intentionally NOT reported.

remaining_47488_scheduler_half.trigger: POST /v1/completions with ignore_eos=true, max_tokens=48 (request does not stop at the block's EOS, so >1 token survives per step)
remaining_47488_scheduler_half.root_cause: AsyncScheduler reserves exactly 1 output placeholder per scheduled step (_update_after_schedule: num_output_placeholders += 1 + spec) but a block-diffusion step emits up to canvas_length=256 tokens, so the placeholder budget underflows whenever more than the reserved count survives the stop-trim.
remaining_47488_scheduler_half.why_first_request_passed: the default-stop request committed EOS at canvas position 0, so _update_request_with_output trimmed to 1 token (within the 1-placeholder reservation) -> assert held.
remaining_47488_scheduler_half.scope: This is the SCHEDULER half of #47488 (num_output_placeholders + num_computed_tokens advance by canvas_length, for both the prefill-block and decode-block). It is beyond the scoped model_runner.py change (which is done + verified). A correct fix must generalize placeholder reservation AND the num_computed_tokens advance to canvas_length; doing so hastily risks silently corrupting generation (per the vLLM-integration skill), so it is left as the precise, live-captured remaining blocker rather than hacked.
remaining_47488_scheduler_half.verdict: Single-block serving where the request stops within block 0 (the realistic DG default-stop path) works live end-to-end (HTTP 200). Multi-token-survival / multi-block serving needs the #47488 scheduler half.
